questions.question: A hiker consumes $2 \cdot 3/4$ liters of water per hour. How many liters will she consume in $2 \cdot 1/2$ hours?
A bird sanctuary has $15 \cdot 3/8$ acres of land. If $2 \cdot 1/4$ acres are reserved for a new species, what percent of the land is reserved?
A car travels $25 \cdot 1/2$ miles per gallon. If gas costs $2 \cdot 3/4$ per gallon, how much will it cost to drive $100$ miles?
A group of friends want to split a bill of $45 \cdot 1/2$. If they split it evenly among $3 \cdot 1/4$ people, how much will each person pay?
A mountain has a height of $8 \cdot 3/4$ kilometers. If a hiker climbs $2 \cdot 1/2$ kilometers, what percent of the mountain has she climbed?
A park ranger measures the length of a trail to be $10 \cdot 1/4$ miles. If she measures it again and gets $10 \cdot 1/2$ miles, what is the percent increase in length?
A group of hikers consume $3 \cdot 3/4$ pounds of food per day. If they have $15 \cdot 1/2$ pounds of food, how many days will it last?
A car's fuel tank can hold $15 \cdot 1/4$ gallons. If $2 \cdot 3/4$ gallons are already in the tank, what percent is filled?

A wildlife reserve has $20 \cdot 1/2$ acres of land for animal habitats. If $3 \cdot 1/4$ acres are for bears and $2 \cdot 1/2$ acres are for wolves, what fraction of the land is left for other animals? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A hiker is planning a trip and needs to calculate her fuel consumption. Her backpack weighs $25 \cdot 3/4$ pounds and she will be hiking for $5 \cdot 1/2$ hours. If she consumes $2 \cdot 1/4$ pounds of food per hour, how many pounds of food will she consume on her trip? Additionally, if she wants to bring $1 \cdot 1/2$ pounds of extra food, what fraction of her backpack's weight will be food? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Remind students to convert between fractions, decimals, and percents where necessary.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use number lines to visualize rational numbers.
Tip 3: Suggest that students use estimations to check their calculations.
